title: Overview of results from NA61/SHINE
abstract: NA61/SHINE is a multipurpose, fixed-target spectrometer operating at the CERN SPS. The studied regime of collision energies, 5.1<\sqrt{s_{NN}}<16.8/27.4 GeV, places the project in-between the two main European heavy ion activities of the coming decade, the continued LHC (0.9<\sqrt{s_{NN}}<14 TeV) and the announced FAIR SIS100 (2.7<\sqrt{s_{NN}}<4.9 GeV) programs. Also, the project partially overlaps with RHIC BES and STAR-FXT (3<\sqrt{s_{NN}}<62.4 GeV, with data taking completed). This contribution gives a subjective summary of the recent results from NA61/SHINE, with particular emphasis on these of greatest importance for the other research programs.
comment: 8 pages, 6 figures. Presented at the XXXII Cracow Epiphany Conference on the recent results from Heavy Ion Physics, January 12-16, 2026, Kraków, Poland
